commit.message:
feat: cluster split & purity detection for mixed-identity clusters

- Add split_cluster() to re-cluster within a single cluster at a tighter
  threshold (0.65), separating mixed-identity faces into sub-clusters
- Largest sub-cluster keeps original cluster_id + person_id; smaller
  sub-clusters get new IDs with person_id cleared for user review
- Add compute_cluster_purity() and get_impure_clusters() for proactive
  detection of impure clusters
- Add RPC handlers: faces/split-cluster, faces/cluster-purity,
  faces/impure-clusters
- Add IPC bridge + TypeScript types for splitCluster and getClusterPurity
- Add Split button in Inspector toolbar (visible when cluster >= 2 faces)
- Add 4 tests: split mixed, preserve person, no-op pure, purity score

commit.message:
Enforce non-null `fShape` invariant in `G4OCCTSolid` (#185)

* Initial plan

* Enforce non-null fShape invariant in G4OCCTSolid

The constructor and SetOCCTShape() now throw std::invalid_argument when
given a null TopoDS_Shape, establishing a class invariant that fShape is
always non-null. All defensive fShape.IsNull() guards in method bodies
are removed as dead code. Documentation updated throughout to reflect the
invariant. Tests added for null-shape rejection; regression test updated
to use an empty compound shape in place of the now-rejected null shape.

feat: RDP auto-login with lima/lima credentials

Pass username and password as URL params from the instance card.
rdp.html auto-connects when both are provided, skipping the form.
Fix default username from 'admin' to 'lima'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[GATEWAY V2]: Fix availability strategy flows. (#48432)

* [GATEWAY V2]: Fix NPE in ThinClientStoreModel.wrapInHttpRequest for hedged PK-scoped queries

RxDocumentServiceRequest.clone() (used by hedging in executeFeedOperationWithAvailabilityStrategy)
was not copying partitionKeyDefinition. When a PK-scoped query is hedged to a secondary region,
the cloned request has partitionKeyInternal (PK value) but null partitionKeyDefinition (PK schema).
ThinClientStoreModel.wrapInHttpRequest needs both for client-side EPK computation, causing NPE:

  Cannot invoke PartitionKeyDefinition.getPaths() because partitionKeyDefinition is null
  at PartitionKeyInternalHelper.getEffectivePartitionKeyBytes
  at ThinClientStoreModel.wrapInHttpRequest

Root cause: RxDocumentServiceRequest.clone() did not copy partitionKeyDefinition.
This only manifests in GW V2 (thin client) because GW V1 never reads partitionKeyDefinition
from the request - it serializes the PK as a JSON header and lets the server resolve EPK.
Point operations are unaffected because they clone RequestOptions (not the request) for hedging.

Fix (defense in depth):
- Primary: ThinClientStoreModel overrides performRequestInternal to reactively resolve
  partitionKeyDefinition from the collection cache when missing on the request. Includes
  logger.warn for production observability when the fallback triggers.
- Secondary: RxDocumentServiceRequest.clone() now copies partitionKeyDefinition.

Test coverage:
- Unit test: cloneShouldPreservePartitionKeyDefinition (verified: fails without fix, passes with)
- E2E test: PK-scoped query with fault-injected response delay triggering hedging on GW V2
  (verified: NPE without fix, passes with fix)
- Wired FaultInjectionWithAvailabilityStrategy test suite (FITests_read/write/query/readMany/readAll)
  into fi-thinclient-multi-region and fi-thinclient-multi-master test groups for holistic coverage.

* Fix ConsistencyLevel.valueOf for wire-format values like BoundedStaleness

TenantWorkloadConfig.getConsistencyLevel() called valueOf(toUpperCase()) which
fails for wire-format values like 'BoundedStaleness' -> 'BOUNDEDSTALENESS'
(enum name is BOUNDED_STALENESS). Now falls back to matching the display name.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Skip DIRECT-only query FI tests under thin client mode

FITests_queryAfterCreation test configs use ONE_SECOND_DURATION timeouts
designed for DIRECT mode. Thin client forces GATEWAY mode which has higher
latency through the proxy, causing 408 timeouts. Skip DIRECT configs when
thin client is enabled until proper GATEWAY variants are added.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Skip only baseline DIRECT tests under thin client; keep availability strategy tests running

FI tests with availability strategy (hedging) must still run under thin client since
query/readAll hedging is core functionality being validated. Only skip DIRECT mode test
configs that have no availability strategy (baseline AllGood tests with tight timeouts).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert FI test skip logic — availability strategy works for all connection modes

Availability strategy (hedging) is connection-mode agnostic. Tests should run under
thin client without skipping, regardless of whether the config specifies DIRECT or GATEWAY.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Skip DIRECT mode FI tests under thin client + HTTP/2 in base execute()

When thin client and HTTP/2 are enabled, DIRECT mode is forced to GATEWAY.
Skip DIRECT test configs centrally in the base execute() method rather than
in each individual FI test file. GATEWAY configs with availability strategy
continue to run — hedging is connection-mode agnostic.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: drop HTTP/2 requirement from thin client DIRECT skip

Thin client forces GATEWAY mode regardless of HTTP/2 setting. The ThinClient
MultiMaster job runs with http2.enabled=false, so the previous condition
(isThinClientEnabled && isHttp2Enabled && DIRECT) never triggered.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Enable HTTP/2 for ThinClient_MultiMaster pipeline job; restore full skip condition

The ThinClient_MultiMaster job was missing -DCOSMOS.HTTP2_ENABLED=true in
tests.yml, so thin client was running without HTTP/2. Added the flag to match
the ThinClient base job. Restored the full 3-way skip condition:
isThinClientEnabled && isHttp2Enabled && connectionMode == DIRECT.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add explanatory comment for DIRECT skip; enable HTTP/2 for ThinClient_MultiRegion

Added detailed comment explaining why DIRECT mode tests are skipped under
thin client + HTTP/2: requests route through gateway proxy, DIRECT timeouts
are too tight, GATEWAY + availability strategy tests still run.

Also enabled -DCOSMOS.HTTP2_ENABLED=true for ThinClient_MultiRegion job
to match ThinClient and ThinClient_MultiMaster.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix RegionalRoutingContext map key issues breaking cross-region routing in thin client mode

RegionalRoutingContext objects were used as keys in CaseInsensitiveMap, which
hashes via toString(). Since toString() includes the mutable thinclientRegionalEndpoint,
two issues arose:

1. equals()/hashCode() included thinclientRegionalEndpoint which is set AFTER map
   insertion in addRoutingContexts(), corrupting map lookups for point operations.
   Fix: equals()/hashCode() now only use the immutable gatewayRegionalEndpoint.

2. getRegionName() creates new RegionalRoutingContext(gatewayEndpoint) for lookups,
   but these have thinclientRegionalEndpoint=null while stored keys have it set.
   CaseInsensitiveMap.convertKey() calls toString() producing different strings,
   so lookups fail and fallback returns first write region for ALL regions.
   This caused availability strategy hedging to target the same region twice
   (no actual cross-region hedging), and affected circuit breaker, session
   container, and diagnostics region identification.
   Fix: Use HashMap instead of CaseInsensitiveMap for RegionalRoutingContext keys,
   so lookups use equals()/hashCode() which only depend on gatewayRegionalEndpoint.

3. Deferred regionByEndpoint map insertion to after thinclient endpoints are set,
   and added null check on regionalRoutingContext before setThinclientRegionalEndpoint.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix PPAF GATEWAY tests for thin client: replace thinProxy with gatewayProxy

ThinClientStoreModel uses RNTBD binary encoding for request/response,
which is incompatible with the standard HTTP mock responses created by
setupHttpClientToReturnSuccessResponse(). Replace thinProxy with
gatewayProxy in PPAF GATEWAY mock paths so data requests use the same
mocked HttpClient with standard HTTP encoding. PPAF retry/failover
logic is transport-agnostic, so this correctly tests the failover
behavior without requiring RNTBD-encoded mock responses.

Also add COSMOS.THINCLIENT_ENABLED and COSMOS.HTTP2_ENABLED system
properties to fi-thinclient-multi-region and fi-thinclient-multi-master
Maven profiles to ensure Configs.isThinClientEnabled() returns true in
forked JVM.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Increase e2e timeout by 500ms for thin client FI tests

Thin client adds overhead for container + partition key range cache

- Add Configs.isHttp2Enabled() check alongside isThinClientEnabled()
  in all thin client guards (PPAF, circuit breaker, assertions)

  toString only depend on gatewayRegionalEndpoint

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix ChangeFeedProcessor isStarted race from CI optimization

PR #48259 replaced Thread.sleep(sleepTime) with a poll loop that
exits early when receivedDocuments >= createdDocuments. When
createdDocuments is empty (incrementalChangeFeedModeToFullFidelity
non-start-from-beginning path), the loop exits immediately — before
the async start() has completed. Add a separate poll for isStarted()
with a 10s timeout to handle this race.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix broken benchmark WorkflowTest CLI tests

readMyWritesCLI and writeThroughputCLI pass CLI args like
-serviceEndpoint that were removed from Configuration.java in the
benchmark refactor. Convert to TenantWorkloadConfig-based tests.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix ChangeFeed processor flaky test regression

Revert the poll-instead-of-sleep optimization in
validateChangeFeedProcessing (introduced in PR #48259). The poll
loop exits as soon as documents arrive, but the CFP needs the full
sleep duration to fully initialize internal state. Without the full
sleep, the processor appears started but hasn't populated its
internal state machine, causing downstream assertions to fail.

Also change stop() from subscribe() (fire-and-forget) to block()
to ensure leases are fully released before the caller starts
another processor on the same lease container.

Also increase fullFidelityChangeFeedProcessor.start() timeout from
10s to 50s since it needs to acquire leases from the just-stopped
incremental processor.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(battle-royale): add is_private flag to prevent queue matchmaking on private rooms

- Add `is_private` boolean column to `battle_royale_rooms` (default false)
- `createRoom()` defaults to `isPrivate = true` — invite-code rooms are queue-blind
- `joinQueue()` filters `is_private = false` so randoms only join public rooms
- `joinQueue()` creates a public room (`isPrivate = false`) when no open room exists
- Expose `isPrivate` in `BRPublicView` / `BRRoomRow` types (backend + frontend)
- Migration replaces partial index with one scoped to public waiting rooms

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(redis): Upstash compatibility — eager connect, halve duel cron

- Remove lazyConnect from RedisService; add onModuleInit ping so the
  TLS handshake to Upstash happens once at startup rather than on the
  first in-flight game request
- Reduce duel-timeout cron from every 15s to every 30s, halving Redis
  lock commands (~11,500 → ~5,700/day) to stay within Upstash free tier

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Initial plan

* Fix Logger: use local level constants, pass correct string level to plenary.log, fix instance call in init.lua

Fix Railway OOM: cap cluster workers with MAX_WORKERS env var (default 2)

Previously os.cpus().length was used, which returned 48 on Railway's
build machine, causing the process to be killed immediately on startup
due to memory exhaustion.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Document VERB-na-ah nominalized locatives in clause report

Add section explaining that VERB-na-ah patterns are nominalized noun
phrases, not clause-chaining devices like converbs (VERB-in).

Key findings:
- VERB-na creates a nominal (e.g., muh-na 'seeing' → 'sight')
- The noun takes case like any noun: muh-na-ah 'at the seeing'
- Most common: X muhna-ah 'in X's sight' (192 occurrences)
- Subject tracking: independent (vs same-subject for converbs)
- No changes needed to analyzer - correctly handled as VERB-NMLZ-LOC

This clarifies the distinction between:
- Converbs (VERB-in): clause chaining, same-subject
- Nominalizations (VERB-na-ah): noun phrases, adjuncts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Regenerate reports after -in CVB disambiguation

Update postposition, relator, NP structure, switch reference, and
transitivity reports to reflect the new CVB (converb) vs ERG
(ergative) distinction for -in suffix.

Key change: VERB-in forms are now glossed as CVB (converb) marking
same-subject sequential action, while NOUN-in remains ERG (ergative).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Prompt:
In the newly created package, create the Wizard component.

Note: these docs mention `Wizard.Step` and `Wizard.Footer`. DO NOT create these yet. They will be created later

The `@leafygreen-ui/wizard` is a general-purpose, multi-step page template, designed to create guided in-app flows and wizards:

Based on the MultiStepWizard component in MMS, and intended to be used in the Product Deletion template.

Feature Overview:
- Takes in all Steps in the flow as children.
- Renders the appropriate content for the current step
- Internally handles step changing (with optional external control)

Non-goals:
- We will not be implementing this across MMS (MultiStepWizard is currently used in 26 files)
 - This will not support different url routes per step

Wizard component
The root flow component. Controls the rendering of the appropriate step based on a controlled prop, or uncontrolled internal state.

Example
```tsx
const [activeStep, setActiveStep] = useState(0)

<Wizard activeStep={activeStep}>
  <Wizard.Step
    title="Step 1"
    description={<>Some description with a <Link>link</Link></>}
  >
    Some Content. Lorem ipsum dolor.
  </Wizard.Step>
  <Wizard.Step />
  <Wizard.Step />
  <Wizard.Footer
    backButtonProps={{ onClick: setActiveStep(x--) }}
    cancelButtonProps={{}}
    primaryButtonProps={{ onClick: setActiveStep(x++), variant: 'danger', disabled }}
  />
</Wizard>
```

Props:
```ts
activeStep?: number;
onStepChange?: (step: number) => void
showStepper?: boolean; // omit for v1
```

State:
`[activeStep, setActiveStep] = useState<number> // if none provided as a prop`

Events:
- `onStepChange` : fired when the activeStep changes
  - this should still fire when controlled?

Rendering:
- Renders the appropriate Step based on the activeStep prop/state
- Renders the Footer element, with enabled/hidden buttons based on the activeStep
  - If activeStep === 0, hides back button
- Injects setActiveStep into Back and Primary buttons (if uncontrolled)

* Creates WizardStep and WizardFooter

Prompt:

The Footer and Step components have been scaffolded.

Create both components with the following spec:

Step:

A single Step in the multi-step flow. Must be rendered within a Wizard.
```ts
title: ReactNode;
description: ReactNode;
children: ReactNode;
```

Footer:
The footer element for the Wizard. A wrapper around LeafyGreen `FormFooter`, but allows us to optionally inject event handlers into the buttons.

```
backButtonProps: ButtonProps;
cancelButtonProps: ButtonProps;
primaryButtonProps: ButtonProps;
```

commit.message:
fix: prevent wheel scroll-down from resetting _userScrolledAway during streaming

Touchpad/precision-scroll devices fire interleaved up+down deltas in
the same gesture. Combined with the timing of programmatic ScrollToEnd,
a wheel-down event could arrive with distanceFromBottom=0 (because
ScrollToEnd just ran), immediately resetting _userScrolledAway=false
and undoing the user's scroll-up intent.

Remove the false-resetting path from OnUserWheelScroll entirely.
Re-enabling auto-scroll is now only handled by:
- ResetAutoScroll() when user sends a message
- OnScrollChanged detecting scrollbar drag to within 8px of bottom

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add -in converb (CVB) vs ergative (ERG) disambiguation

Round 196: Distinguish -in suffix based on morphological context:
- VERB-in → CVB (converb): Same-subject sequential marker
- NOUN-in → ERG (ergative): Transitive subject marker

Key insight: When -in attaches directly to a verb stem (no nominalizer),
it marks same-subject sequential action (converb), not ergative case.
This is distinct from the ergative -in on nouns.

Changes:
- Add CVB disambiguation at suffix stripping (lines 19679-19696)
- Add CVB disambiguation in recursive suffix chain (lines 19820-19863)
- Update ~65 compound entries from ERG to CVB for verb-based forms
  (REFL-*-ERG → REFL-*-CVB, *-CAUS-ERG → *-CAUS-CVB, etc.)
- Add regression tests for -in disambiguation

Test cases:
- bawlin → make-CVB (verb + converb)
- gamin → land-ERG (noun + ergative)
- kihongin → REFL-open-CVB (reflexive verb + converb)

Coverage maintained at 100% (830,739/830,741 tokens)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix pr_monitor_stop not closing viewer (#42)

* Fix pr_monitor_stop not closing viewer by writing STOPPED to log

The single-monitor stop path called CancelPolling() and Dispose() but
never wrote a STOPPED line to the log file. The viewer watches for this
line to know when to close, so it kept running indefinitely.

- Write STOPPED log line in single-monitor pr_monitor_stop (matching
  the stop-all path)
- Add StopPermanently() that cancels without creating a new token
- Check IsStopped flag in poll loop as belt-and-suspenders
- Clarify SKILL.md: stop monitoring should call pr_monitor_stop,
  not restart via pr_monitor_start

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Make StopPermanently and Dispose thread-safe with Interlocked.Exchange

Use Interlocked.Exchange to null out _pollCts atomically in both
StopPermanently and Dispose, preventing ObjectDisposedException
when concurrent stop/shutdown paths race.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Separate log write from shutdown cleanup in NotifyShutdown

Move StopPermanently/Dispose into a separate try-catch from the
STOPPED log write so session cleanup always executes even if the
log file write fails due to IO errors.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Separate log write from cleanup in stop-all loop

Same pattern as NotifyShutdown: isolate the STOPPED log write so
StopPermanently/Dispose always runs even if the file append fails.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Make CancelPolling thread-safe with Interlocked.Exchange

Use Interlocked.Exchange to atomically swap the CTS, dispose the
old one, and guard against ObjectDisposedException. Also skip if
IsStopped to prevent recreating a CTS after permanent shutdown.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Enhance POS tagging for sentence-level analysis

Added comprehensive word class detection to get_word_class():
- AGR: Agreement markers (a, ka, na, i - verbal proclitics)
- V: Verbs (via VERB_STEMS, TAM suffixes, directionals, common glosses)
- SUBORD: Subordinators (ciangin, hangin, bangin, dingin, etc.)
- SFIN: Sentence-final markers (hi, hiam, hen)
- NEG: Negation (lo, kei)
- PRO: Pronouns (amah, eite, etc.)
- N.PROP: Proper nouns (detected via all-caps gloss)

Added new dictionaries:
- SUBORDINATORS: Temporal, causal, conditional clause markers
- PRONOUNS: Personal pronouns

Enhanced verb detection to recognize:
- Compound verbs (khen-khia → divide-exit)
- Verbs with TAM/directional suffixes
- Common verb meanings in glosses

Added tests/test_pos_tagging.py with 20 tests covering all word classes.

This lays groundwork for clause-level parsing and Form I/II tracking.
